LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT DOUGLAS EVANS, M.D.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE MEDICAL NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD HER MEDICAL NEGLIGENCE CLAIM AS TWO CAUSES OF ACTION - ONE FOR WRONGFUL DEATH AND ONE BROUGHT AS A SURVIVAL ACTION. ORDER SIGNED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES AND ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING.) JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER MELANIE GHENO, CSR NO. 7489 (302/EHG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES - DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR WILLFUL MISCONDUCT AND NEGLECT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE FACTS SET FORTH IN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT ("SAC") ALLEGE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PROFESSIONAL NEGLIGENCE. THE ELDER/DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE ACT "EXCLUDES LIABILITY FOR PROFESSIONAL NEGLIGENCE." (SABABIN V. SUPERIOR COURT (2006) 144 CAL.APP.4TH 81, 88; SEE ALSO COVENANT CARE, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT (2004) 32 CAL.4TH 771, 781.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-29-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. SHAWN D. TILLIS OF WINER & MCKENNA, LLP, TEL. NO. 1-510-433-1000 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CORINNA E. MEISSNER OF THE HASSARD BONNINGTON LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-288-9800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES - Defendants' demurrer to Plaintiff's second cause of action for Willful Misconduct and Neglect is sustained without leave to amend. The facts set forth in the Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") allege a cause of action for professional negligence. The Elder/Dependent Adult Abuse Act "excludes liability for professional negligence." (Sababin v. Superior Court (2006) 144 Cal.App.4th 81, 88; see also Covenant Care, Inc. v. Superior Court (2004) 32 Cal.4th 771, 781.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-29-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. SEAN TELLIS APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CORINNA E. MEISSNER OF THE HASSARD BONNINGTON LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-288-9800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS' MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT - Defendants' motion to strike punitive damages and attorneys' fees is granted. Plaintiff cannot recover punitive damages on her negligence claim, as Plaintiff does not have a Court order permitting her to claim punitive damages, as required by CCP sec. 425.13. Defendant's motion to strike prejudgment interest is denied. Under Civil Code 3287(a) "[a] person who is entitled to recover damages certain, or capable of being made certain by calculation, and the right to recover which is vested in the person upon a particular day, is entitled also to recover interest thereon from that day, except when the debtor is prevented by law, or by the act of the creditor from paying the debt'" Here, Plaintiff's damages are capable of being made certain by calculation because she is claiming loss of income and decedent's annual income is calculable. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-12-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT DOUGLAS EVANS, M.D.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT as to the third cause of action for Dependent Adult Abuse is sustained without leave to amend. The facts set forth in the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") allege a cause of action for professional negligence. The Elder/Dependent Adult Abuse Act "excludes liability for professional negligence." (Sababin v. Superior Court (2006) 144 Cal.App.4th 81, 88; see also Covenant Care, Inc. v. Superior Court (2004) 32 Cal.4th 771, 781.) The demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the medical negligence cause of action. Plaintiff may pursue her medical negligence claim under two distinct theories - wrongful death and survival. However, as pled, the FAC alleges three distinct causes of action. Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead her medical negligence claim as two causes of action - one for wrongful death and one brought as a survival action. Order signed. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302.

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-12-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT DOUGLAS EVANS, M.D.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Granted without leave amend. As stated in the ruling on demurrer, Plaintiff has not alleged a cause of action for Dependent Adult Abuse. As such, Plaintiff's claim for punitive damages and attorneys' fees based on that cause of action is stricken. Plaintiff also cannot recover punitive damages on her negligence claim, as Plaintiff does not have a Court order permitting her to claim punitive damages, as required by CCP sec. 425.13. Order signed. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302.
